home.title: SGraph AI — AI Agents That Build, Use, and Secure the Same Platform
home.hero.title: AI agents that build, use, and secure the same platform.
home.hero.lead: SG/Send is a zero-knowledge encrypted file sharing platform, built entirely by AI agents working in parallel. The same agents that built it use it every day.
home.platform.title: SG/Send — Zero-Knowledge File Sharing
home.platform.desc: Files are encrypted in your browser using AES-256-GCM before upload. The server stores only encrypted ciphertext. Your decryption key never leaves your device.
home.platform.callout: We literally cannot read your files.
home.feature.encryption.title: Browser Encryption
home.feature.encryption.desc: AES-256-GCM encryption runs entirely in your browser via the Web Crypto API. Files are encrypted before they ever leave your device.
home.feature.zero_knowledge.title: Zero Knowledge
home.feature.zero_knowledge.desc: The server never sees your plaintext data, your file names, or your encryption keys. By design, we cannot access your content.
home.feature.serverless.title: Serverless Pricing
home.feature.serverless.desc: Runs on AWS Lambda — you only pay for what you use. No idle servers, no minimum commitments, scales to zero.
home.team.title: Meet the Team
home.team.subtitle: 18 AI agents. One human. Everything in public.
home.agent.sherpa.title: Sherpa
home.agent.sherpa.desc: Guides users through SG/Send and answers questions. The first point of contact for anyone who needs help.
home.agent.ambassador.title: Ambassador
home.agent.ambassador.desc: Represents SGraph AI to the outside world. Translates technical capability into clear communication.
home.agent.architect.title: Architect
home.agent.architect.desc: Designs the system topology, API contracts, and data models. Ensures every component fits together coherently.
home.agent.librarian.title: Librarian
home.agent.librarian.desc: Maintains the knowledge base, master index, and reality document. The single source of truth for what actually exists.
home.agent.dev.title: Dev
home.agent.dev.desc: Writes the application code, implements features, and fixes bugs. Works in Python with FastAPI, Type_Safe, and Memory-FS.
home.agent.appsec.title: AppSec
home.agent.appsec.desc: Reviews every change for security vulnerabilities. Verifies the zero-knowledge model holds under all conditions.
home.built.title: Built in Public
home.built.desc: SG/Send is developed using an agentic model: 18 specialized AI agents collaborate under human oversight, with every decision, review, and line of code tracked in the open.
pricing.title: Pricing — SG/Send
pricing.hero.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.hero.lead: Zero-knowledge encrypted file sharing from one penny per file. No subscriptions. No hidden fees. Pay for what you use.
pricing.tier.free.label: Early Access
pricing.tier.free.name: Free
pricing.tier.free.desc: Join the Early Access Program and evaluate SG/Send with free credits.
pricing.tier.starter.label: Starter
pricing.tier.starter.name: Credits
pricing.tier.starter.desc: 500 encrypted file transfers at just $0.01 per file. Valid for 3 months.
pricing.tier.selfhosted.label: Enterprise
pricing.tier.selfhosted.name: Self-Hosted
pricing.tier.selfhosted.desc: Run SG/Send in your own AWS account with your own encryption keys.
pricing.encryption.text: Every tier uses the same zero-knowledge encryption. Files are encrypted in your browser using AES-256-GCM before upload. The server stores only encrypted ciphertext. We literally cannot read your files.
pricing.how.title: How it works
pricing.how.subtitle: Get started in minutes. No software to install.
pricing.how.step1.title: Get your access token
pricing.how.step1.desc: Join Early Access for free or buy a Starter Pack. You’ll receive a unique access token via email.
pricing.how.step2.title: Upload and encrypt
pricing.how.step2.desc: Visit send.sgraph.ai, enter your token, and upload a file. Encryption happens in your browser — the file is encrypted before it leaves your device.
pricing.how.step3.title: Share the link
pricing.how.step3.desc: You get a download link and a decryption key. Share them with your recipient — they decrypt in their browser too.
pricing.how.step4.title: Zero knowledge, always
pricing.how.step4.desc: The server never sees your plaintext data, file names, or encryption keys. By design, nobody but you and your recipient can access the content.
pricing.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
pricing.faq.q1: What counts as one file transfer?
pricing.faq.a1: Each file you upload and encrypt counts as one transfer. Downloading and decrypting by your recipient does not use additional credits.
pricing.faq.q2: What happens when my credits expire?
pricing.faq.a2: Credits are valid for 3 months from purchase. Files you’ve already uploaded remain accessible — only new uploads require credits.
pricing.faq.q3: Can I buy more credits later?
pricing.faq.a3: Yes. When you run low or your credits expire, you can purchase another Starter Pack at any time.
pricing.faq.a4: SG/Send is operated by The Cyber Boardroom Ltd, a company registered in England and Wales. Payments are processed securely via Stripe.

contact.title: Contact — SGraph AI
contact.hero.title: Get in Touch
contact.hero.lead: Have a question, feedback, or want to learn more about SGraph AI? Send us a message and our Sherpa will get back to you.
contact.form.intro: Your message goes to sherpa@sgraph.ai — our Sherpa guides users and answers questions. They are the first point of contact for anyone who needs help with SG/Send.
contact.form.message_placeholder: Tell us what you need help with, what you think, or what you’d like to know...
contact.form.message_hint: 10 – 5,000 characters. Be as detailed as you like.
contact.form.email_hint: If you’d like a reply, leave your email address.
contact.form.screenshot_hint: If applicable, attach a screenshot to help explain your question.
contact.modal.title: Contact the Sherpa
contact.modal.intro: Your message goes to sherpa@sgraph.ai — our Sherpa guides users and answers questions.
product.title: SG/Send — Zero-Knowledge Encrypted File Sharing — SGraph AI
product.hero.title: Your files, your keys, your privacy
product.hero.subtitle: SG/Send encrypts files in your browser before upload. The server stores only encrypted data. Your decryption key never leaves your device.
product.how.title: How it works
product.how.subtitle: Three steps. Zero knowledge. Complete privacy.
product.step.encrypt.title: Encrypt
product.step.encrypt.desc: Your browser generates a unique encryption key and encrypts your file using AES-256-GCM via the Web Crypto API. This happens entirely on your device.
product.step.upload.title: Upload
product.step.upload.desc: Only the encrypted ciphertext is uploaded. The server never sees your file’s contents, name, or decryption key.
product.step.share.title: Share
product.step.share.desc: Share the download link. The decryption key is embedded in the URL fragment (#) — it never reaches our servers.
product.compare.title: What makes SG/Send different
product.compare.subtitle: A side-by-side comparison with traditional file sharing services.
product.usecases.title: Built for real-world use
product.usecases.subtitle: From one-off transfers to enterprise data rooms.
product.usecase.documents.title: Secure Document Transfer
product.usecase.documents.desc: Send contracts, financials, and legal documents. Zero-knowledge encryption means nobody between sender and recipient can read the contents.
product.usecase.datarooms.title: Data Rooms
product.usecase.datarooms.desc: Create encrypted data rooms for due diligence, M&A, or board materials. Enterprise security without enterprise pricing.
product.usecase.passwords.title: Password Sharing
product.usecase.passwords.desc: Share credentials, API keys, and secrets through an encrypted channel instead of plain-text email or chat.
product.usecase.agents.title: AI Agent Coordination
product.usecase.agents.desc: AI agents use SG/Send to exchange encrypted files and keys, with full audit trails and PKI-verified identity.
product.enterprise.title: Enterprise-grade features
product.enterprise.subtitle: Everything you need for secure file operations at scale.
product.feature.aes.title: AES-256-GCM Encryption
product.feature.aes.desc: Military-grade authenticated encryption. Every file gets a unique key generated in your browser.
product.feature.zk.title: Zero-Knowledge Architecture
product.feature.zk.desc: The server stores only ciphertext. No file names, no plaintext, no decryption keys ever touch the backend.
product.feature.selfhost.title: Self-Host Option
product.feature.selfhost.desc: Run SG/Send on your own AWS account. Your infrastructure, your data, your control.
product.feature.audit.title: Immutable Audit Trail
product.feature.audit.desc: Git-based audit logging. Every transfer, access, and event is recorded in an append-only, tamper-evident trail.
product.feature.tokens.title: Token-Based Access Control
product.feature.tokens.desc: Fine-grained permissions with scoped access tokens. Control who can upload, download, and manage transfers.
product.feature.vaults.title: Personal Encrypted Vaults
product.feature.vaults.desc: Persistent encrypted storage for each user. Files stay encrypted at rest with keys only you control.
product.feature.rbac.title: Data Rooms with RBAC
product.feature.rbac.desc: Role-based access control for collaborative spaces. Set viewer, editor, and admin permissions per room.
product.feature.api.title: API + MCP Integration
product.feature.api.desc: Full REST API plus Model Context Protocol support. Integrate SG/Send into any workflow or AI agent pipeline.
product.feature.deploy.title: 7 Deployment Targets
product.feature.deploy.desc: Lambda, Docker, Fargate, GCP Cloud Run, EC2, AMI, or CLI. One codebase, deploy anywhere.
product.pricing.title: Pay for what you use. Nothing when you don’t.
product.pricing.subtitle: Traditional secure file sharing charges $10,000–35,000 per project. Modern alternatives cost $180–600/month whether you use them or not.
product.pricing.desc: SG/Send runs on serverless infrastructure. When nobody is using it, it costs nothing. You pay only for what you use.
product.opensource.title: Open source. Verify everything.
product.opensource.subtitle: SG/Send is open source. Verify our encryption. Audit our code. Run it on your own infrastructure.

arch.aes.desc: AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) is the encryption algorithm used by governments, banks, and militaries worldwide. 256 is the key size in bits — there are 2256 possible keys, making brute force computationally impossible. GCM (Galois/Counter Mode) adds authenticated encryption — it doesn't just encrypt, it also detects if anyone has tampered with the ciphertext.
arch.aes.title: What is AES-256-GCM?
arch.deploy.cli.desc: Command-line interface for scripted transfers, CI/CD pipelines, and power users.
arch.deploy.cli.title: CLI
arch.deploy.container.desc: Docker, AWS Fargate, and GCP Cloud Run. The same application packaged as a container.
arch.deploy.container.title: Container
arch.deploy.lambda.desc: Primary deployment. Two Lambda functions behind Lambda Function URLs. Direct HTTPS endpoints, no API Gateway needed.
arch.deploy.lambda.title: Lambda
arch.deploy.server.desc: EC2 instances and AMI builds. For teams that need full control of the runtime environment.
arch.deploy.server.title: Server
arch.deploy.subtitle: One codebase, seven deployment targets grouped into four patterns.
arch.deploy.title: Deployment Targets
arch.flow.subtitle: Here's exactly what happens when you send a file through SG/Send.
arch.flow.title: The Encryption Flow: Step by Step
arch.hero.lead: SG/Send uses AES-256-GCM symmetric encryption, performed entirely in your browser. The server never sees your plaintext data, your file names, or your encryption keys. This page explains exactly how.
arch.hero.title: How the Encryption Works
arch.server.callout: A complete server compromise — full database access, all backups, all logs — reveals nothing about the content of transferred files. The ciphertext is computationally indistinguishable from random noise without the key.
arch.server.subtitle: This is the zero-knowledge guarantee. Here is exactly what data exists on the server.
arch.server.title: What the Server Sees vs. What It Doesn't
arch.server_never.desc:
Encryption key — stays in browser, shared via URL fragment
File name — never sent to server
File type — no content-type metadata stored
Plaintext content — only ciphertext ever uploaded
Raw IP address — hashed before storage
arch.server_never.title: Server never sees
arch.server_stores.desc:
Transfer ID — random 12 characters
Encrypted bytes — indistinguishable from random data
IP hash — SHA-256 of IP + daily rotating salt
Timestamp — when the transfer was created
File size — size of the encrypted blob (bytes)
arch.server_stores.title: Server stores
arch.stack.storage.purpose: Pluggable backend: memory, disk, or S3
arch.stack.subtitle: Every layer is chosen for simplicity, security, and zero-dependency deployment.
arch.stack.testing.purpose: No mocks, no patches, real implementations
arch.stack.title: Technology Stack
arch.step1.desc: When you select a file, your browser generates a random 256-bit AES key using the Web Crypto API (crypto.subtle.generateKey). This uses the operating system's cryptographically secure random number generator. The key exists only in your browser's memory.
arch.step1.title: Key Generation
arch.step2.desc: A 12-byte Initialization Vector (IV) is generated randomly (crypto.getRandomValues). The IV ensures that encrypting the same file twice produces different ciphertext. The IV is not secret — it is prepended to the encrypted output and sent with the ciphertext.
arch.step2.title: IV Generation
arch.step3.desc: The file is encrypted in the browser using crypto.subtle.encrypt with the AES-GCM algorithm, the generated key, and the IV. The output is ciphertext + a 128-bit authentication tag. The authentication tag is GCM's integrity guarantee — if anyone modifies even one bit of the ciphertext, decryption will fail.
arch.step3.title: Encryption
arch.step4.desc: Only the encrypted output (IV + ciphertext + auth tag) is uploaded to the server. The server receives a blob of random-looking bytes. It assigns a Transfer ID (12 random characters) and stores the ciphertext. No file name, no content type hint, no key — just encrypted bytes.
arch.step4.title: Upload
arch.step5.desc: The sender gets a download link. The decryption key is placed in the URL fragment (the part after #). URL fragments are never sent to the server by the browser — this is defined in RFC 3986. The sender shares this link through any channel they choose (email, chat, in person).
arch.step5.title: Link Sharing
arch.step6.desc: The recipient opens the link. Their browser downloads the encrypted bytes from the server, extracts the key from the URL fragment, separates the IV from the ciphertext, and decrypts using crypto.subtle.decrypt. If the auth tag doesn't match (tampering detected), decryption fails. The server never participates in decryption.
arch.step6.title: Decryption
arch.symmetric.callout: The key insight: if you control where the key is generated and where it travels, symmetric encryption gives you perfect confidentiality. SG/Send generates the key in your browser, and the key never touches the server.
arch.symmetric.subtitle: SG/Send uses symmetric encryption — the same key encrypts and decrypts the file. This is the simplest and fastest encryption model, and when done correctly, the most secure for file transfer.
arch.symmetric.title: Symmetric Encryption: One Key, Two Operations
arch.system.admin_lambda.desc: Admin-only function for token management, analytics, server stats, and MCP admin tools. Requires authentication. Separate Lambda = separate security boundary.
arch.system.admin_lambda.title: Admin Lambda (Authenticated)
arch.system.cdn.desc: Static assets, caching, SSL termination, and WAF. Lambda URLs provide the HTTPS endpoints directly — no API Gateway needed.
arch.system.cdn.title: CloudFront CDN
arch.system.memfs.desc: All storage goes through an abstraction layer. Application code never knows if the backend is in-memory, on disk, or S3. Same codebase across all 7 deployment targets.
arch.system.memfs.title: Memory-FS (Storage_FS)
arch.system.subtitle: Two Lambda functions, one storage layer, one CDN. Simple by design.
arch.system.title: System Architecture
arch.system.user_lambda.desc: Public-facing function that handles file transfers, health checks, presigned uploads, personal vaults, and MCP integration. Accessed via Lambda Function URL behind CloudFront.
arch.system.user_lambda.title: User Lambda (Public)
arch.why_symmetric.desc: Symmetric encryption uses one key for both encryption and decryption. This is different from asymmetric encryption (like RSA) which uses a key pair. For file transfer, symmetric is the right choice: it's fast, well-understood, and the Web Crypto API provides a battle-tested implementation in every modern browser.
arch.why_symmetric.title: Why Symmetric?
agent.sherpa.card1.desc: Designs and maintains the first-time user experience. From landing on the site to completing a first encrypted file transfer, the Sherpa owns every step of the journey.
agent.sherpa.card1.title: User Onboarding
agent.sherpa.card2.desc: Identifies and documents every point where users get stuck, confused, or drop off. Friction logs are filed as issues and prioritized for the Dev and Designer agents.
agent.sherpa.card2.title: Friction Logging
agent.sherpa.card3.desc: Monitors how users navigate the product. Which paths do they take? Where do they hesitate? The Sherpa turns usage patterns into product improvement recommendations.
agent.sherpa.card3.title: Trail Observation
agent.sherpa.card4.desc: Answers questions about encryption, file transfers, vaults, and data rooms. The Sherpa explains zero-knowledge concepts in plain language that anyone can understand.
agent.sherpa.card4.title: Support & Guidance
agent.sherpa.cta.subtitle: Use SG/Send to share encrypted files directly with this agent.
agent.sherpa.cta.title: Send a file to the Sherpa
agent.sherpa.details.title: Details
agent.sherpa.email.title: Email
agent.sherpa.hero.lead: The Sherpa guides individual users through the product and owns onboarding, friction logging, and trail observation. Every onboarding flow, support interaction, and piece of user-facing guidance passes through the Sherpa.
agent.sherpa.hero.title: Sherpa
agent.sherpa.keys.pending: Key generation pending — public keys will be published when PKI infrastructure is deployed. See keys.json for the machine-readable directory.
agent.sherpa.keys.subtitle: Use these keys to verify the Sherpa's identity and encrypt messages to this agent.
agent.sherpa.keys.title: Public Keys
agent.sherpa.overview.subtitle: The Sherpa is the first point of contact for anyone who needs help with SG/Send.
agent.sherpa.overview.title: What the Sherpa Does
agent.sherpa.since.title: Active Since
agent.sherpa.team.title: Team
agent.architect.card1.desc: Defines where each component starts and stops. The two-Lambda architecture (User + Admin), the Memory-FS storage abstraction, and the client-server encryption boundary are all Architect decisions.
agent.architect.card1.title: System Boundaries
agent.architect.card2.desc: Owns the 73 HTTP endpoints across the platform. Every route, request schema, and response format is specified by the Architect before Dev writes a line of code.
agent.architect.card2.title: API Contracts
agent.architect.card3.desc: Designs the Type_Safe schemas for transfers, vaults, rooms, tokens, and audit events. All models use osbot-utils Type_Safe — never Pydantic, never raw dicts.
agent.architect.card3.title: Data Models
agent.architect.card4.desc: Ensures the zero-knowledge model holds at every layer: client-side AES-256-GCM encryption, no plaintext on server, no file names stored, hashed IP addresses with daily salt.
agent.architect.card4.title: Encryption Guarantees
agent.architect.cta.subtitle: Use SG/Send to share encrypted files directly with this agent.
agent.architect.cta.title: Send a file to the Architect
agent.architect.decision1.desc: Separate User and Admin Lambda functions with different security boundaries. The User Lambda is public-facing. The Admin Lambda requires authentication. A compromise of one cannot access the other.
agent.architect.decision1.title: Two Lambdas, Not One
agent.architect.decision2.desc: All storage goes through Storage_FS. Application code never knows if the backend is in-memory, on disk, or S3. This enables the same codebase to run across all 7 deployment targets without changing a line of application logic.
agent.architect.decision2.title: Memory-FS Abstraction
agent.architect.decision3.desc: Direct HTTPS endpoints via Lambda Function URLs instead of API Gateway. Simpler topology, lower latency, lower cost. CloudFront sits in front for CDN, caching, and WAF.
agent.architect.decision3.title: Lambda URLs, Not API Gateway
agent.architect.decision4.desc: The decryption key is placed in the URL fragment (#), which browsers never send to the server. This is how the zero-knowledge model works for shared links — the server literally cannot intercept the key.
agent.architect.decision4.title: Key in URL Fragment
agent.architect.decisions.subtitle: Major decisions the Architect has made and why.
agent.architect.decisions.title: Key Architectural Decisions
agent.architect.details.title: Details
agent.architect.email.title: Email
agent.architect.hero.lead: The Architect defines and guards the boundaries between components, owns API contracts and data models, and ensures all technology decisions serve the zero-knowledge encryption guarantee. Thinks in interfaces and contracts, never implementations.
agent.architect.hero.title: Architect
agent.architect.keys.pending: Key generation pending — public keys will be published when PKI infrastructure is deployed. See keys.json for the machine-readable directory.
agent.architect.keys.subtitle: Use these keys to verify the Architect's identity and encrypt messages to this agent.
agent.architect.keys.title: Public Keys
agent.architect.overview.subtitle: The Architect is responsible for the structural integrity of the entire platform.
agent.architect.overview.title: What the Architect Does
agent.architect.since.title: Active Since
agent.architect.since.value: v0.1.x — one of the founding agents
agent.architect.team.title: Team
agent.ambassador.card1.desc: Analyzes the competitive landscape — from enterprise file sharing (Citrix ShareFile, Box) to privacy-focused alternatives (Tresorit, SpiderOak). Identifies positioning opportunities where SG/Send's zero-knowledge model is a genuine differentiator.
agent.ambassador.card1.title: Market Research
agent.ambassador.card2.desc: Plans and executes outreach campaigns. Targets communities that care about encryption, privacy, and open source — security professionals, privacy advocates, regulated industries, and AI teams.
agent.ambassador.card2.title: Outreach & Growth
agent.ambassador.card3.desc: Defines how SG/Send presents itself to the market. The Ambassador ensures the messaging is clear: zero-knowledge is not a feature, it's the architecture. Serverless pricing means zero cost when idle.
agent.ambassador.card3.title: Brand Positioning
agent.ambassador.card4.desc: Builds trust through transparency. The open-source repo, the public agent directory, the "built in public" model — the Ambassador turns these into growth drivers, not just engineering practices.
agent.ambassador.card4.title: Community Building
agent.ambassador.cta.subtitle: Use SG/Send to share encrypted files directly with this agent.
agent.ambassador.cta.title: Send a file to the Ambassador
